required_header.download_date: ClinicalTrials.gov processed this data on February 2026
required_header.link_text: Link to the current ClinicalTrials.gov record.

clinical_study.brief_title: Adjuvant FOLFOX in Stage III MSI-H Colon Cancer with KRAS Mutation

brief_summary.textblock: This study examines outcomes of adjuvant FOLFOX chemotherapy in patients with microsatellite instability-high (MSI-H) Stage III colon adenocarcinoma with node-positive disease. Despite current debate around the benefit of 5-FU-based therapy in MSI-H Stage II disease, this study focuses on the node-positive Stage III population where oxaliplatin-based regimens remain standard of care.

detailed_description.textblock: Patients with resected sigmoid or colon adenocarcinoma, Stage IIIB or IIIC (pT3-T4, pN1-N2, M0), with MSI-H tumor status and KRAS mutation are enrolled. All patients receive FOLFOX (5-fluorouracil, leucovorin, oxaliplatin) every 2 weeks for up to 12 cycles. CEA monitoring and CT imaging are performed at regular intervals during and after treatment. The study evaluates 3-year disease-free survival as the primary endpoint and prospectively collects data on peripheral neuropathy related to oxaliplatin exposure.

clinical_study.study_design: Intervention Model: Single Group Assignment, Primary Purpose: Treatment

criteria.textblock:
Inclusion Criteria:
- Histologically confirmed colorectal adenocarcinoma, colon primary (sigmoid, descending, transverse, or ascending colon)
- Pathologic Stage III disease (pT3 or pT4, pN1 or pN2, M0) following curative intent surgical resection with negative margins
- 1 to 6 positive regional lymph nodes
- Microsatellite instability-high (MSI-H) status confirmed by IHC and/or PCR
- KRAS mutation detected by NGS (any codon)
- BRAF wildtype
- HER2-negative by IHC
- Adequate post-surgical recovery: initiation of adjuvant chemotherapy within 12 weeks of resection
- Age 18 to 65 years
- ECOG performance status 0 or 1
- CEA elevation at diagnosis (greater than 3 ng/mL) with post-operative decline
- Adequate organ function: ANC greater than or equal to 1.5, platelets greater than or equal to 100 (Grade 1 thrombocytopenia acceptable), creatinine less than or equal to 1.5x ULN, AST/ALT less than or equal to 2x ULN
Exclusion Criteria:
- Rectal cancer (tumor below peritoneal reflection) as primary site
- BRAF V600E mutation
- MSS (microsatellite stable) or MSI-low tumor
- Metastatic disease (Stage IV) at diagnosis or restaging
- Prior pelvic radiation therapy
- Hereditary Lynch syndrome (pathogenic germline mutation in MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, PMS2, or EPCAM)
- Pre-existing Grade 2 or higher peripheral neuropathy
- Active inflammatory bowel disease
- Prior oxaliplatin-based chemotherapy
